label: Wide 2BR — horizontal split: front band (ensuite|standard|living), rear band (master|hall+common|stacked dining+kitchen)

adjacencies.note: ensuite at far-left of FRONT band, master at left of REAR band — share the front/rear-band boundary wall directly above ensuite
master's east face is hall's west — master accessed via hall
standard accessed via living (both in FRONT band, side by side)
common bath's west face is hall's east — bath door faces the hall
hall ends at dining — single bridge from the private notch to the public LDK
shared plumbing wall between common bath and kitchen — concentrates wet stack in the rear band
common's south face touches dining's portion that wraps around — shared wall, no door (door faces hall)
living and dining share the front/rear-band boundary wall (cross-band open plan flow)
dining stacks above kitchen — kitchen at rear edge, dining in front of it within the rear band

notes: Authored from the user's layout spec for wide shell:
- Buildable shell split horizontally into two bands (front + rear).
- FRONT band (street side): ensuite at the far-left end, then standard
bedroom, then living room (left to right).
- REAR band: master bedroom (left), then a small hall, then common T&B,
then dining + kitchen STACKED inside the right end of the rear band
(dining in front of kitchen, kitchen against rear wall).
Access flows:
- Master accessed via the hall (master ↔ hall direct).
- Standard accessed via living (cross-band shared wall).
- Common bath has its door on the hall side (private circulation); its
other wall touches the kitchen for the wet-core plumbing.
- Single hall-mouth bridge from hall to dining (private → public).
Front_to_rear_stacks chain enforces the band partition: each FRONT-band
room is constrained to be in front of its REAR-band counterpart, and
within the rear band, dining is in front of kitchen (so they stack).
Lot-depth note: 11x5 (16x9 lot) is extremely tight for this 8-room
program — dining + kitchen stacked within a 2.5 m rear-band depth runs
into the hard min area + aspect cap for dining_room. Test results:
16x9  (5 m depth) → infeasible or marginal
16x10 (6 m depth) → solves, dining and kitchen compact
16x11 (7 m depth) → comfortable
